content.problemStatement:
Chef is making NNN atomic soups numbered 111 through NNN. Each soup is either a base atomic soup or a composition of other atomic soups. For each valid iii, if soup iii is not a base soup, one unit of this soup is made by mixing together one unit of soup ci,1c_{i, 1}ci,1‚Äã, one unit of soup ci,2c_{i, 2}ci,2‚Äã, and so on to one unit of soup ci,Pic_{i, P_i}ci,Pi‚Äã‚Äã. Note that these PiP_iPi‚Äã soup units are not necessarily from distinct soups and that we obtain only 111 unit of soup by mixing PiP_iPi‚Äã units.

Due to their structure, two units of the same base atomic soup always cancel out and vanish when mixed together. Therefore, we define the base decomposition of a soup as the set of all base atomic soups that need to be used an odd number of times when making the resulting soup (i.e. a base soup bbb belongs to the base decomposition if we need to use an odd number of units of soup bbb in total). The base decomposition of a sequence of atomic soups is the base decomposition of the soup created by mixing together one unit of each of these soups.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given a sequence of Chef's soups s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sKs_1, s_2, \dots, s_Ks1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sK‚Äã; you must decide whether it is possible to choose two disjoint subsequences (one of which may be empty) of this sequence which have the same base decomposition. Two subsequences are disjoint if there is no index iii such that each subsequence contains soup sis_isi‚Äã.
constraints: 1‚â§ci,j<i1 \le c_{i, j} \lt i1‚â§ci,j‚Äã<i for each valid i,ji, ji,j
1‚â§si‚â§N1 \le s_i \le N1‚â§si‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains an integer PiP_iPi‚Äã; if Pi‚âÝ0P_i \neq 0Pi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=0, it is followed by a space and PiP_iPi‚Äã space-separated integers ci,1,ci,2,‚Ä¶,ci,Pic_{i, 1}, c_{i, 2}, \ldots, c_{i, P_i}ci,1‚Äã,ci,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ci,Pi‚Äã‚Äã.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains an integer KKK followed by a space and KKK space-separated integers s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sKs_1, s_2, \dots, s_Ks1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sK‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing a string with length QQQ. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string should be '1' if it is possible to choose required subsequences in the iii-th query or '0' otherwise.
